paper.title: Reformulating lipid nanoparticles for organ-targeted mRNA accumulation and translation

formulations.ionizable_lipid: nAcx-Cm series (degradable ester-core ionizable lipids, e.g. 6Ac1-C12)
formulations.helper_lipid: NONE (3-component LNP: IL + cationic lipid + PEG-lipid)
formulations.targeting_details: 3-component LNP with NO cholesterol and NO phospholipid. Cholesterol removal prevents liver accumulation. Cationic lipid addition drives lung targeting.
formulations.formulation_notes: Key paradigm shift: cholesterol and phospholipid are DISPENSABLE for LNP functionality. Removing cholesterol prevents liver accumulation. 3-component (IL + cationic + PEG) achieves lung targeting. 4-component (IL + cationic + cholesterol + PEG) also targets lung but with some liver spillover.

formulations.targeting_details: Standard 4-component LNP with conventional composition. Liver-targeted. Targets hepatic endothelial cells (not just hepatocytes).
formulations.formulation_notes: 6Ac1-C12 LNPs with standard 4-component composition target liver, but primarily to endothelial cell subsets rather than hepatocytes. This is unusual and clinically relevant.

results.liver_notes: Cholesterol removal eliminates liver accumulation. 3-component (no cholesterol, no phospholipid) + cationic lipid = exclusive lung mRNA translation. 4-component (no phospholipid but with cholesterol) + cationic = lung-selective but with some liver.

experiments.notes: Paradigm-breaking finding: cholesterol and phospholipid are dispensable. Cholesterol is what drives liver accumulation via ApoE/cholesterol/LDL-R axis. Removing it breaks the liver sink.

key_findings_for_project: PARADIGM SHIFT: Cholesterol and phospholipid are DISPENSABLE for LNP functionality
Cholesterol removal prevents liver accumulation — breaks ApoE/cholesterol/LDL-R axis
3-component LNP (IL + cationic + PEG) achieves exclusive lung targeting with no liver signal
Novel degradable ester-core ionizable lipids (nAcx-Cm series) — biodegradable design
Liver-targeting with standard composition primarily reaches hepatic endothelial cells, not hepatocytes
BM NOT measured — lung/liver/spleen/kidney/heart organ panel only
Implications for HSC project: cholesterol-free LNPs might avoid liver sink entirely

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Different approach. Suggests cholesterol removal could potentially be combined with antibody targeting for maximal liver de-targeting.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Complementary mechanism: cholesterol removal for passive liver de-targeting + CD117 for active HSC targeting could be powerful combination.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Cholesterol is a major component of Kim's formulations. This paper suggests removing it entirely as a liver de-targeting strategy.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: No BM data. Cannot place on Pareto.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Cholesterol-free LNP is a novel design parameter not in your current feature matrix. Could be encoded as cholesterol_mol_pct = 0.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Medium-high. The principle (cholesterol = liver sink) is important for rational design. nAcx-Cm ionizable lipids are novel but structures are in the paper.

limitations_and_caveats: Lung-focused study — unclear if cholesterol-free LNPs can also deliver to BM
nAcx-Cm ionizable lipids are novel and may not be in external databases
Cholesterol removal may affect LNP stability for larger cargo (gene editors)
Chinese academic group (Zhejiang University) — different from Dahlman/Siegwart labs
